name: Easter Mints Kids Can Make
recipeInstructions:
This is a no-cook recipe the children can mix with their hands.
Flavor it with any of the liquid flavorings in the supermarket, such as strawberry and lemon. If desired, divide the mixture into three portions and add a few drops of food coloring to tint one yellow, one red, and one green. Knead a small amount of flavoring into each portion separately. This recipe makes about 1 1/2 lbs of candy.
Help the children measure all ingredients into the large bowl. They can take turns stirring with the wooden spoon until it becomes too stiff to stir. Then they should knead by hand, continuing until the dough is smooth and no longer sticky.
Instruct each child to place their paper plate upside down on a flat surface and write their name at the bottom using pencils. Encourage them to use both uppercase and lowercase letters. Assist them in holding their pencils correctly.
Provide each child with a portion of dough on their plate. They can pinch off pieces, roll them into balls, and press lightly with a fork to create fancy butter mints. Children who cannot roll the candy into balls can make snakes, cut the snakes into pieces, and press those pieces with a fork instead. They may eat the pieces by pressing them with a fork.
Allow the mints to sit on the plates for 30 minutes in the refrigerator until they become firm. Easter Mints taste even better the following day if possible. Once set, cover with plastic wrap and store in the refrigerator.
